title: L4: Selection
description: Using comparison operators, if statements, and logical operators to make decisions in Python.

steps.title: Do Now Activity
steps.content:
Do Now Activity

5 minutes - from last lesson

Answer these quick questions from the input lesson before learning selection.

steps.title: Selection
steps.content:
Selection

Selection lets a program make a decision. In Python, we usually start with an if statement.

score = 75
if score >= 50:
    print("Passed")

Click run to see what happens

The indented line only runs when the condition is True.

steps.title: Level 19 - Choose Comparison Operators
steps.content:
Level 19 - Choose Comparison Operators

Choose the operator that matches each meaning.

Choose the correct comparison operator:

steps.title: Typing Comparison Operators
steps.content:
Typing Comparison Operators

Comparison operators compare the value on the left with the value on the right.

10 > 5 # greater than
10 == 10 # equal to
10 != 3 # not equal to

Click run to see what happens

Use = to assign a value, but == to compare two values.

steps.title: Level 20 - Type Comparison Operators
steps.content:
Level 20 - Type Comparison Operators

Type the correct comparison operator for each meaning.

Type the correct comparison operator:

steps.title: If Statements
steps.content:
If Statements

An if statement starts with if, then a condition, then a colon.

if temperature > 30:
    print("Hot day")

Click run to see what happens

The colon is required. The code inside the if statement must be indented.

steps.title: Level 21 - Choose If Statements
steps.content:
Level 21 - Choose If Statements

Choose the correct full Python if block.

Choose the correct full Python if block:

steps.title: Typing If Statements
steps.content:
Typing If Statements

Be precise when typing an if statement: variable, operator, value, colon.

if lives != 0:
    print("Keep playing")

Click run to see what happens

String values need quotes inside comparisons, such as if role == "player":.

steps.title: Level 22 - Type If Statements
steps.content:
Level 22 - Type If Statements

Type the correct full Python if block.

Type the correct full Python if block:

steps.title: Logical Operators
steps.content:
Logical Operators

Logical operators combine or flip conditions.

if age >= 11 and age <= 18:
    print("School age")

Click run to see what happens

and needs both sides to be True. or needs at least one side to be True. not flips True and False.

steps.title: Level 23 - Choose Logical Operators
steps.content:
Level 23 - Choose Logical Operators

Choose the correct Python logical operator.

Choose the correct Python logical operator:

steps.title: Typing Logical Operators
steps.content:
Typing Logical Operators

Python uses words for logical operators: and, or, and not.

if not game_over:
    print("Continue")

Click run to see what happens

Do not use JavaScript symbols like && or || in Python.

steps.title: Stage 4 Ready
steps.content:
Ready for Stage 4

You have now practised every skill that appears in the Stage 4 exam.

Compare values

score >= 50

Write if blocks

if lives != 0:
    print("Keep playing")

Use and

if age >= 11 and age <= 18:
    print("Allowed")

Use or / not

if not game_over:
    print("Continue")

Click run to see what happens

steps.title: Exam 4: Selection
steps.content:
Exam 4: Selection

Work through all questions. This checks comparison operators, if statements, and logical operators.

steps.title: Endless Practice
steps.content:
Endless Practice

Random questions from across the Selection lesson. Answer 5 questions correctly in a row to complete this step.

Streak: 0 Best: 0

quizQuestions.q: Which operator means greater than or equal to?
Which operator means not equal to?

What does this print?

score = 50
if score >= 50:
    print("Pass")

What does this print?

temperature = 30
if temperature > 30:
    print("Hot")
Which full if block checks whether lives is not zero?
Which line correctly compares role with the text player?
Which logical operator needs both conditions to be True?
Which logical operator needs at least one condition to be True?

What does this print?

age = 12
if age >= 11 and age <= 18:
    print("Allowed")

What does this print?

coins = 3
if coins > 5 or coins == 3:
    print("Open")
Store a score of 50. Write a full if block that prints Pass when the score meets the pass mark.
Write a full if block that checks whether lives is not zero and prints a message inside the block.
Store an age of 12. Write a full if block using and that prints Allowed only when age is in the 11 to 18 range.
Store coins as 3. Write a full if block using or that prints Open when coins is either more than 5 or exactly 3.
Write a full if block that uses not with game_over and prints a message inside the block.
